Okay, let's see. I need to figure out how many of the 3x1x1 waffles I need to eat so that the remaining ones can fit into a 2x2x4 box without breaking any. Hmm.

Each waffle is 3x1x1, so the volume of one waffle is 3*1*1 = 3 cubic units. There are seven waffles in total, so if I don't eat any, their total volume would be 7*3 = 21 cubic units. But the box is only 16, so obviously they can't all fit by volume. But volume alone isn't the only consideration here—how they fit in the box matters too.

Since the waffles are 3x1x1, their length of 3 units needs to fit in one of the box's dimensions. The box is 2x2x4. The 3-unit length can't fit in the 2-unit sides, but can it fit in the 4-unit side? Let's see.

So each waffle must be placed so that the 3-unit side is aligned with the 4-unit side of the box.
